Control Title: Automated Tools to Support Information Location
Implementation Action: Locate Enterprise policy and procedures for automated information location and apply them to EEEEE
Action Detail: Confirm the business application follows the enterprise configuration management policy and the procedures for identifying and documenting information location on on-prem servers, storage systems, and application databases.

Control Title: Automated Tools to Support Information Location
Implementation Action: Document the automated tools used by EEEEE to identify Confidential and Secret data, including regulated personal data, on system components
Action Detail: Record the tools, the information types they detect, the system components scanned, and the outputs produced for the application’s production environment.

Control Title: Automated Tools to Support Information Location
Implementation Action: Update EEEEE system security documentation to include information location outputs and related data mappings
Action Detail: Reflect the automated information location results in the system security plan, privacy plan, system design documentation, PII inventory documentation, and data mapping documentation as applicable.

Control Title: Automated Tools to Support Information Location
Implementation Action: Retain change control records and system component inventory evidence showing where automated information location has been applied in EEEEE
Action Detail: Keep records that demonstrate the application’s production servers, storage systems, and application databases are covered by the automated identification process and that the results are available for compliance evidence.

Implementation Action: Locate the enterprise configuration management policy and procedures and identify the requirements for information location and data action mapping that apply to EEEEE
Action Detail: Confirm the business application team’s obligations for documenting where information is located and how data actions are mapped within the application boundary.

Implementation Action: Develop and document a map of system data actions for EEEEE covering collection, generation, transformation, use, disclosure, retention, and disposal of personally identifiable information
Action Detail: Include the discrete data actions, the PII elements involved, the system components performing each action, and the owners or operators of those components.

Implementation Action: Update EEEEE system design and compliance documentation to include the data action map as an overlay or equivalent system design artefact
Action Detail: Ensure the map is captured in the system security plan, privacy plan, system design documentation, PII inventory documentation, or other relevant records used for the application.

Implementation Action: Coordinate with the relevant privacy and security stakeholders to validate the completeness of the EEEEE data action map and the identified system components
Action Detail: Confirm the mapped data actions and component ownership reflect the application’s actual processing and operating model.

Implementation Action: Locate the enterprise configuration management policy and procedures for digitally signed software and firmware components and confirm EEEEE is in scope
Action Detail: Identify the approved enterprise requirements that prevent installation unless signatures are verified using organisation-approved certificates.

Implementation Action: Document EEEEE-specific installation procedures that require verification of digital signatures before applying application software updates, patches, service packs, and drivers
Action Detail: Cover the exact update types used by EEEEE and state that installation must be blocked unless the signature is verified against an approved certificate.

Implementation Action: Record the approved certificate verification method and the personnel responsible for checking signed components for EEEEE
Action Detail: Specify who performs the verification, what evidence is checked, and where the approval status of certificates is confirmed.

Implementation Action: Update EEEEE change control and deployment records to require evidence of signature verification for each software update release
Action Detail: Ensure release and change records capture the signed component check before deployment is authorised.

Implementation Action: Maintain an EEEEE component inventory that identifies the software update, patch, service pack, and driver types subject to signed-installation checks
Action Detail: List the applicable component categories so installation controls can be applied consistently to all in-scope software components.

Implementation Action: Verify that EEEEE installation processes prevent deployment of unsigned software components unless digital signature verification against an approved certificate is completed
Action Detail: Test the operational process using a representative update path and retain evidence that unsigned components are not installed.
